body: Hi I know everyone is different and I get zero commission from saying this, I just wanted to help if I can. This koprez sleeve has helped me loads. Supports my foot while standing all day. I have lost one and over the last few days my foot with one on is better than the foot without it on after a long day.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: How do you tolerate standing all day? I've been dealing with PF for over 6 years and unfortunately at this point I'm starting to think it might not ever go away. My job is starting to get busier and will require me to stand on concrete for long shifts for the foreseeable future. Unfortunately I have to wear safety toe boots for my job. Any advice?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: These vionic slippers have been the most helpful thing for my plantar fasciitis the past 7+ years, but they have recently been discontinued. Has anyone found a comparable arch support slipper? They have a very firm high arch support which feels better than any orthodics inserts or special shoes I have ever tried.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I need to buy shoes for a Memorial at which I'm speaking and singing. Since my onset of PF which has resolved with care, cortisone and orthotics, I've been living in athletic shoes and cowboy boots. What are some nice dress shoe options for women that you can recommend if they won't kill my feet?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Women's ballet flat style shoes with arch support? Do they exist??
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Hi, I'm a healthcare professional and I am on my feet ALL DAY. I've been searching for comfortable shoes because my New Balance 9060 are not supportive. My feet ache all the time. PLEASE drop your recommendations down below.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I need comfy, supportive dressy sandals I can wear to a wedding with a dress. Any recommendations? I usually wear Hokas and Oofos for comfort.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Oofos. Hi has anyone purchased oofos and how have you found them. Thinking of these for the house.

I got some Oofos slides with adjustable velcro on the top for Christmas - asked for them on my gift list because I started getting plantar fasciitis in my feet I think from walking barefoot when in the house so trying to keep good arch support shoes on my feet in the house. I like the Oofos for the arch support but on the right foot the slide is rubbing on my bunion and making it sore - anyone have any ideas on something I could put there?

So podiatrist appt today. Not ingrown. He said it was curved tho and cut it down. Not throbbing now, so we will see. When he cut it, most of the fungus is gone. The other toe still has some fungus tho. I'm still using anti fungal and soaking. So hoping maybe I got it early enough. He said he thought my feet looked good tho so here's hoping.

Hi has anyone had all their toenails removed with bed before? I would like some photos once all healed. I am looking to get this done to mine as I have tried everything including the tablets 3 times and come back worse each time.

An old friend tells me that tea tree oil cleared up his toenail fungus. I looked on amazon.com and there are dozens of brands to choose from. Can someone recommend one for me to try. I can't tolerate fragrances and perfumes so I'd prefer one with no additives.

Has anyone experienced stabbing nerve pain going into their big toenail? This started happening to me a few days ago and it's torture. The only things that help are putting Biofreeze on it and taking Percocet. I have been filing my nail down really thin and putting Nonyx nail gel on it and antifungal cream for 2 years. Maybe doing these things for so long irritated a nerve in my toe? I went to urgent care today and they gave me Gabapentin.

Hello, new here. Ten years, three podiatrists and one foot nurse later, here I am. I was told all this time I had thickened toenails due to repeated trauma, ill fitting shoes etc. Previous tests negative until the most recent one. I've tried everything over the years, listerine, epsom salts, all the topicals, ACV, Urea. Doctor unwilling to prescribe oral, says liver risk outweighs benefits. Recently started Toe Fx (Canada but I think in US soon). After significantly grinding the nails down and applying it daily I am seeing improvement.

Thank you for adding me. Went to a podiatrist today and he wanted to remove the nail, which now after joining this group I am glad I didn't. I asked him what the redness was on my skin and he was noncommittal. My nurse practitioner first thought it was cellulitis, so I did a round of doxycycline which of course was no help. Does toenail fungus make your toe painful and swollen? I am going to try the compounded topical lamisil, because I cannot do oral meds. Has anyone had success with that?

Does it ever get better? Every time I make some progress then it comes back 10x worse. I'm in so much pain but I'm terrified to take the pills prescribed. I'm so sensitive to medication I know it's going to really mess me up. I just wish I could heal this naturally but I'm losing hope that will ever happen. Right now it's the worst it's ever been and it's spread all the way back into my cuticle even and my entire toe just throbs.

Previous PF. Ruptured achilles 6 months ago. PF back badly. Ankle so tight. Doing all exercises recommended by both private and NHS physio. Grateful for all help. Trying isometric strengthening myself now. Yoga everyday to work on hips and more. NHS podiatry making insoles. I am so confused about where to go for advice to get a full overview of my situation if that is possible. Also which footwear for my two issues.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: FACTS! LET THESE NOT HAPPEN TO YOU. I calmed my pain using foamy shoes, slippers and orthotics. Yes indeed. That is easy. But now my back hurts so bad. I have bulging discs. I feel like my whole system is collapsing. Now I have peroneois longus tendinosis and achilles tendinosis because those gadgets weakened my foot muscles. Now I am stuck with this miserable condition. I am calming my pain while I am also deteriorating.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I went to see a Sports Physio yesterday for the first time and thought I would share. She believes I have had Plantar Fasciitis which has now morphed into an Insertional Achilles problem. She thinks because I have been adjusting my running style to compensate. She identified an imbalance whereby my left side is weaker than the right but said that calf raises probably are not the answer as I already have strong calves through cycling.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Hi everyone! Thank you for the add! I'm having surgery on Thursday for bunion and hammertoe. Also, they will be taking a bit of bone from my heel to fill a negative space where gout has eroded the bone. I wasn't worried until I began researching. Now I'm a bit terrified.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Hey guys! I am 27 from the UK. My doctor has said I can get surgery and said waiting is 8/12 weeks. I also suffer from Morton's neuroma in both feet. So far I've had 2 steroid injections and booked in for radio frequency as they have both been ineffective. I had a scary thought last night that once my bunions have been operated on and I'm all bandaged up in tight bandages, this will send my Morton's neuroma into a frenzy with the pressure. I struggle to wear narrow shoes never mind weeks of bandage. Has anyone had surgery with Morton's neuroma?
source_group: Forefoot forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: I've always had wide feet. I could manage to squeeze into fashionable trainers etc but would rub. Anyway, I was pregnant last year and my left foot mainly just went whomp and the tailors bunion appeared! It doesn't give me pain really unless it was in a shoe. I'm basically only really able to wear crocs at this point. I'm pregnant again and worried it will get worse. I'm 31 and I feel so sad about how I can't wear all of the nice trainers and footwear others get to wear.

body: Anybody heard of burning foot syndrome?

body: Asking for those who have PF on one foot and while PF is getting better, the other foot is showing signs of PF too. My Physio explained to me that it is due to stress and overuse from my other foot compensating for my affected foot with PF. I am at lost and feeling frustrated. Can you please share what you did to overcome this and not progress?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I am extremely frustrated at this moment. I believe the steroid injection has caused more damages. I had injection in June, then different issues started in October, worse in November. I have got pain to arch instead of heel, swelling to whole foot, no improvement after 4 weeks on moon boot, my foot is so weak, waiting for MRI results, my podiatrist mentioned about another injection, I just wish my foot could go back to before steroid injection.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I currently feel so sad and frustrated because I wanted to go enjoy this beautiful new year day weather we're having but I couldn't walk more than 15-20 minutes (1 lap around a pond). I truly hate PF! I've been dealing with it for almost 2 years! I wouldn't wish this on my worst enemy!
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Why do some of you not like the surgery or don't suggest it? I quit my job and I can't walk, I'm getting so frustrated.

Just been for my pre op and my blood pressure was high. She did it 3 times! So I've now got a blood pressure monitor at home and got to check it for 7 days (my op is in 2 weeks) just hoping it was a stress thing. I know I've put on weight so that probably doesn't help but I don't want my op to be cancelled or postponed.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Hey everyone. Ty for having me in the group. I am nervous about my foot/bunions. Its pretty severe. How bad is surgery and how is healing time?

body: Right bunion foot surgery 1/19. I'm scared to wet it and get infected. I been showering with shower chair and covering foot. Doctor said I can wet and removed the strips. I tried removing strips (circle blue) days ago and noticed bleeding and pain, therefore I stopped. Now incision is painful. I been covering with ace band to protect area rubbing slippers when walking around the house. How long took you to full shower standing and get surgery foot wet? Did you remove the strips?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Dear lovely friends, I am in a bit of a dilemma and would value advice. After the Scarf and Akin surgery in October went wrong and the surgeon decided not to correct the hammertoes and to leave the big toe floating and pushing against the second toe, I am scared to do revision surgery. It would be lapidus surgery and hammertoe surgery on three toes. He is a very good surgeon and expert in his field but I don't have that much pain now, more than before the surgery but not constant.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: I am 19 days post op of 3D Lapidus Lapiplasty on my right foot. I had a follow up with my surgeon and I was scared because the x-ray showed a gap at the fusion site. My surgeon assured me it was fine and healing as expected.

I have access to a shower chair, crutches and a walker to help. I bought a foot raising pillow which will keep legs elevated. Bathroom has shower and toilet rails. Ill be staying at Mums house with daughter opting to work from home for 3 weeks to help.

source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Hi! Im an ultra endurance runner with a 200+ miles trail race coming up in June. I got injured, not sure how, in December. Kept milking my runs. Then, I went no running and only stationary cycling in February. Just got a prp shot 10 days ago and in more pain now than I was! What's everyone's thoughts on being able to race in 10 weeks? Manage it through pacing and suffer through? Pain currently 2/10. Hiked 3 miles today, no flare up but tender.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Does it ever get better? Everytime I make some progress then it comes back 10x worse. I'm in so much pain but I'm terrified to take the pills prescribed. I'm so sensitive to medication I know it's going to really mess me up. I just wish I could heal this naturally but I'm losing hope that will ever happen. Right now it's the worst it's ever been and it's spread all the way back into my cuticle even and my entire toe just throbs.

Summer Shoes/Sandals with Toe Nail Fungus - The warmer weather is approaching. Those of you who've had issues last summer, what type of shoes/sandals did you wear in the summer? I've always worn open toe sandals in the past and nail polished pedicures. I would like to let my toes breathe instead of wearing closed toe shoes/sneakers but without nail polish, I'm embarrassed to wear sandals this summer.

My friend Sarita is on a mission to end foot pain. I've watched my friend Sarita struggle for over 11 years with a painful foot deformity. I saw her try every bulky, uncomfortable brace out there, and I saw how much it hurt her just to go for a walk. Instead of staying stuck, she spent the last few years inventing her own solution: toe spacer socks that actually feel good and work with normal shoes.

For about a year I have had increasing pain on lateral side of one foot and below 4th and 5th metatarsal joint. I know the fat pads are practically non existent and have relied on memory foam insoles to provide some degree of comfort. Now I discover my foot is overly supinated and I use heel wedges to compensated with some degree of success. I really don't want to undergo surgery as I am an anesthetic risk from bronchiectasis.

I am looking for insole suggestions. Please help me. I have Morton's toe and flat feet which means my second and third toe joint are what strikes first and no arch to support and I've had chronic stress fractures because of it. I don't have insurance right now and I'm trying to find a good insert I can put in my shoes so I can exercise without causing too much pain.

Hi everyone, just joined the group as I'm just looking for some support from people who get it. I feel like family and friends just don't, which is understandable. I basically had a full foot reconstruction. They realigned my heel, rebuilt my arch, fused part of my midfoot, corrected my big toe, straightened my 4th toe, and put in several screws to hold everything in place. It was quite a big op to fix my collapsed foot and stop it getting worse. I got run over by a bus as a child which caused the damage.

Ordered new custom orthotics for my horrifically flat feet. I've been waiting 7 weeks. They took moulds of my feet and then they were sent from uk to Australia where they are made. Hoping they make the difference for ankle tendonitis. I've been off past 2 months with this - physio says muscles are very weak. The exercises I was given hurt but I'm doing them - dread going back to work.
Recommended new custom orthotics which fit and physio for peroneal tendonitis due to flat feet. Had physio few years back on other foot which found tough - anyone any tips?

Has anyone had nerve conduction test? First of all I've had foot pain for years dealing with plantar fasciitis. I get flare ups from time to time from overuse but it's manageable. In November I broke my fifth metatarsal. Spent 8 weeks in a boot. Just now getting around well. Now I get this weird tingling sensation in my arch. Dr recommended a nerve conduction test. It's expensive and was hoping to get some insight on how it works. Anyone familiar with?

People with diagnosed diabetic neuropathy in their feet do your feet go through different stages of temp, tingling vs sharp pain, different areas of constant pain vs shooting pain or do these symptoms happen all the time 24/7?

Hi I have had foot pain since February and although I go to the hospital next week I'm still unsure. The pain is different each day and the severity. I get lots of pain where the blue is in the photo alongside burning, hotness and stiffness. It can be either bad when I wake or ok but the pain by bed time is terrible. I even have trouble getting off the sofa. Hurts to drive, walk, sit and lie down. Pain goes from a 3 to 12 out of ten. Any ideas if it's small fibre neuropathy or PF. I do have neuropathy in my head and face. Thanks
4 1/2 months post op - my scars thickened a bit. They suggested Aquaphor and massage. Anyone else have other suggestions?
comments: Jade roller
Scar tape. They are little sheets you put over the scar. It has helped me a bit but I still have pain where my scar is at.

body: I have Morton's Neuroma due to my bunions so it's time I get my bunion surgery I've needed since I was 16. I'm 37 now. Can someone tell me more about it? I know very little.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Here are my before and after pics. Arthritis was more problem than bunion, and also Morton's Neuroma removal. Photo at 10 weeks. Walking with a bit of pain, so much better. No more stabbing at night!
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Healing & recovery must-haves! I just finished my 3rd surgery on my feet, this time to remove hardware from one, and bone spurs and a neuroma removed from the other. Both had previous lapidus bunion removals with fusion, plus one had 2 toes shortened & the other had 2 hammertoes repaired. These 3 items have kept me much more pain free through all my surgeries, and have helped scarring immensely. For nerve pain, I take Alpha Lipoic Acid and Tumeric Curcumin daily. For scarring & bruising I start using Castor Oil right after stitches come out. They work wonders for me! If you are struggling, just know that it does get better and you will get your life back. Hang in there! Much love to everyone
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: 6 weeks post-op for 1st toe MTP fusion, 2nd toe hammertoe correction, neuroma removal from between second and third toe, and capsule release of 3rd toe. I'm still wearing a surgical shoe and have been allowed to heel walk since surgery. I hope to be allowed to wear shoes, walk normally and ride a stationary bike after my x-ray on Wednesday. I've also been driving since going off the percoset after the first week post-op. I truly believe that the skill of my surgeon has made for a very manageable surgery. If you're in the Phoenix area and need a surgeon, Stanley Graves, M.D. is who I recommend.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Has anyone been diagnosed with Morton's Neuroma? Possibly even a result of bunions? I was referred to MRI due to bunion pain and pain in metatarsal area. MRI suggested MN in the 2nd/3rd webspace. Curious to hear your experiences and what helped. Symptoms developed over 3 months' time
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Hi has anyone had all there toe nails removed with bed before? I would like some photos once all healed I am looking to get this done to mine as I have tried everyone including the tablets 3 times and come back worse each time

I had a partial ingrown toenail removed earlier today. I did not know it could *possibly* involve chemicals. I'm breastfeeding and am worried they used Phenol. Is there anyway to tell if this looks like they did? I called but of course the dr left already. Checked my chart and nothing at all is listed.

It gives me a giggle to know I have fungus at 25.. heh. Ranttt I've had it for 2 years now. With nothing helping or curing it. I just recently used some kerasal to make my nail mushy to get rid of the icky under the nail plate. I have TRIED multiple different things. That one medication everyone raves about from the doctors. Nail polish type things. Oils. Creams. The lot. I have come to the conclusion... DIG AT THE TOE AND REMOVE AS MUCH AS I CAN. I think I might schedule an appointment with the podiatrist again... Make them remove my putrid toenail (should of just had them do it months ago when I went to get rid of a ingrown).

Best slip-resistant + waterproof shoes? Also, Hoka Bondi SR reviews for everyday work wear similar to below? My husband works in a meat market, so his feet get wet all day, every day and he's around raw meat and chemicals constantly (when cleaning the market). He has extremely flat feet and plantar fasciitis. He's tried boots (with and without insoles) and tennis shoes (with and without insoles), but nothing has held up or stayed comfortable.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Insoles - ? Needing A LOT of cushioning on my heel to make it through, on concrete floors Have a flat foot and lived in converse n doc Martin's , barefoot for the last 35 years probably ...( yes I know ) Hate arch support to be honest ! Anyone have a suggestion?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Hi! New here. I have flat feet, plantar fasciitis, and I can't remember what else.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Hello everyone... please take a moment to read my story. I truly need your help, even if it's just a word of support. I am 41 years old, and my suffering started two years ago with a mild heel pain, like stepping on a nail. I endured it and continued living my normal life. After a few months, everything suddenly changed... the pain spread to my entire foot — the arch and the ball of my foot — and became unbearable, like tearing sensations and strong electric shocks.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: What type of testing did your podiatrist do? A year ago, went in and the podiatrist asked where my pain was, I pointed to the bottom of my foot right above my heel. He said it was P.F. No tests at all. Gave me a sheet of exercises to do, sold me power step insoles, and prescribed diclofenac sodium. Told me I could only wear Brooks or ASICS tennis shoes and to get a Wide shoe. (I've always worn narrow shoes) At my follow up appointment, no changes. Still stabbing.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I'm almost 2 years po, I recently saw my surgeon because I still have pain in my foot. MRI was taken 6 weeks ago, I have a stress fracture in my tibia. Jesus.

I did my due diligence and went to doctors because this felt different that the regular pf, more intense more pain even while resting, every step terrible pain.

body: An update to my last post. I've been in my new Altra FWD Vias for 4 days now (after 3 days in Crocs while I waited), and for the first time in my life I've worn shoes that give my toes space to spread as I walk. That toe spread is what's healing me. For years, my toes were smushed together in traditional shoes, so my arches never actually worked. Instead, my heels and calves carried the load, which is why my PF flared.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Thought this was a good stretch to share. This is the podiatrist who created Correct Toes spacers, I got mine many years ago when there was only one color available. They are still going strong. Dr. Callahan was the one who opened my eyes to using zero drop shoes with wide toe boxes. However my PF has flared up pretty badly this time around and I've been doing a lot of stretches and forgot about this one.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: So, a fellow groupie - Jenny D Walker - has been posting videos with her doctor who does the EWST therapy, a very high level of shockwave. Touted as a "one and done" procedure for PF. Done with freezing and under ultrasound. I have found out the procedure is not available in Canada, and going to the states (airfare) for the procedure ($3500US) and hotels ($$$$) is not for me. But it is because of her that I went searching for something similar here.

body: So Ive been filing it and pieces are coming off. Last summer the whole nail itself came off but unfortunately grew back same thing. I'm to afraid of the removal I have proclearz, kerasal, clarus but I don't think it will matter if it's this bad unfortunately.
Hello people, I think im in the early stagis of hallux vagus. Runs in family. I walk/jog everyday which is triggering or speeding it. Anything i can do to move my toe back naturally?

body: Any dress shoes good for bunions and flat feet? I wear tennis shoes all the time and occasionally need to dress it up a little.
Vacation with lots of walking. Any recommended shoes? I'm going to do a lot of walking in vacation and I have a severe bunion on my left foot. Any advice or recommendations?
Has anyone tried buying men's sneakers instead of women's to get a wider toe box?

Please can some-one recommend slippers that are wide at the toes, have an arch support (tendonitis) and stay on securely. No memory foam, it hurts my arches and ankles. I've tried trainers indoors but my bunions are so sore and my feet feel fatigued. Crocs hurt, too. I wear Archies flip-flops in Summer. I'm in the UK. Thank you

I haven't been doing feet exercises and my bunion is starting to cross over. I believe the mild discomfort I am having is the rubbing between the two toes. I just put the cotton that is in supplement bottles. I don't know if there is a toe separator to go between the two toes that is made of a soft material that will fit when wearing shoes. Because I have flat feet and other feet issues, podiatrists recommend that I don't go barefoot in the house.

6 weeks post op. Finally healing wel after some healing complications but still alot of swelling. Can move big toe alot better and finally got into a shoe (unfastened) i was due to go back to work in a week but the nature of my job (being on my feet all day) thats not going to happen soon. The big toe is the worst bit. Theres a crease round the bottom of it that feels like I have something wrapped around it and is very uncomfortable.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: I am 6 weeks post op, and one of my incisions is bleeding slightly. I was just able to be non weight bearing yesterday. Has this happened to anyone else?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Nerve healing? I am 8 weeks post op, pretty straightforward bunioectomy. I had a cast for two week non weight bearing, CAM boot for 4 weeks weight-bearing, then was supposed to be in a surgical shoe for two weeks. However, the surgical shoe was causing the ankle that I had operated on in mid-August too much pain, so with the surgeon's approval, I moved into Hokas last week. Once the cast came off, I felt like the nerve zingers really started, but thankfully those zaps are short lived. Right now I still have nerve pain, bruising and swelling.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Bowen therapy! I'd never heard of it till I met someone who'd had it, she showed me her feet, both had had bunions, one had surgery about 10 years ago, the other Bowen therapy and I couldn't tell the difference. I'm 2 sessions in and I can see a noticeable straightening of the toes, I'm so happy. It's a holistic straightening of the skeleton and releasing of the muscles which helps alignment, posture etc. I really think it's worth trying before surgery.

Hi all, almost 3 weeks since my op. I had minimally invasive surgery to correct all 5 toes. Big toe has 3 screws. I have been wearing my boot and walking on my foot since Tuesday (with 1 crutch to assist with balance). Last night when walking, I felt a pop and pain on the side of my foot further down from my baby toe. Today I am finding it very sore to walk on and it just feels like something is off. Has anyone experienced similar?

body: What is everyone's thoughts on a possible link between diet and PF? Just wondering.

Help! Ive been seeing a foot Dr for two years now and am on 600mg lyrica. Here's what an xray say; Minimal calcaneal plantar spurring. Question small chronic erosive changes of the distal 1st metatarsal possibly a sequelae of gout. Joint: No dislocation. Mild 1st MTP joint space degeneration minimal hallux valgus. Soft tissues: No ankle joint effusion.

Greetings! Two questions: 1. Occasional big toe joint pain, but doesn't fit any description of gout that I've found. Throughout any given day, multiple times a day, it will lock up. When it happens, it is pretty painful, but if I force it up by pushing it against something, the pain immediately subsides. No swelling, no redness, no sensitivity of the skin... just the joint. 2. My old orthotic insoles are falling apart. What's the conventional wisdom these days regarding orthotics?

Added another photo, its just getting worse. Anyone know what this is? I have been seeing a podiatrist for a year now. Had a torn plantar plate about 5 or 6 years ago, had that repaired (2nd toe). Started having pain in the ball of my foot (big toe). Went in and they said there was a fractured sesamoid. Was in a boot for a couple months. Ive been in and out trying to figure out what is causing the swelling and pain that is steadily getting worse. Ive done custom orthotics.

Long post I'm sorry!!! I had minor invasive bunion surgery on my right foot in August 2024. Then a second surgery to remove a hardware in October 2024, then another surgery to shave my bone down in December 2024. I still have pain and feel sore all the time. I went to the foot doctor two weeks ago and told him I feel something, almost like a piece of the hardware on the side of my foot, and it's really painful.

I had minimally invasive bunion surgery about 9 months ago and just found out yesterday I'm going to need hardware removal surgery in a few days. Wondering if anyone has experiences they'd like to share about this process/surgery! Thanks!

Has anyone had their hardware removed after a fusion? I'm 7 months post surgery (bunionectomy and fusion) and my foot still feels noticeably tender and stiff. I traded arthritic pain for a new kind of pain. My doctor recommended hardware removal. I'm still coping with irritated nerves, and I'm reluctant to deal with another surgery. I am, however, hopeful that the hardware removal will alleviate my lingering discomfort.

I am almost 1 year post-op bunny next to me with midfoot fusion and I'm going to see my foot doctor about removing the hardware that's been bothering me I'm wondering if anyone has had their hardware removed and how was that.. all the highs and lows... Thank you
I went for my 2 week austin/akin bunionectomy follow up this morning. I get to walk with my boot!
So tired of hiding my feet for 20 years, trying everything and anything. Someone has to know what actually WORKS!!!! — feeling frustrated.
How do you keep your toenails healthy while treating fungus?

It gives me a giggle to know I have fungus at 25.. heh. Ranttt. I've had it for 2 years now. With nothing helping or curing it. I just recently used some kerasal to make my nail mushy to get rid of the icky under the nail plate.

body: After over a year my fungal nail is better! What finally did it was a combination of washing all my clothes with dettol laundry cleaner in. Soaking in Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar and little borax a couple of time a week. Putting H- Nail Fungus and NonyX Nail gel on morning and night and regularly cleaning out under the nail and removing any fungal looking bits

Treatment of house and shoes. Hello! I am looking to know how to completely eradicate athlete's foot. It has been spread from my boyfriend to me and my son. We have carpet throughout the whole apartment. We also use the same tub sometimes. It's starting to stress me out. My boyfriend wears work boots that he leaves at work. I've heard of the apple cider vinegar soaks are effective. What are the ratios? 1 cup of vinegar to 3 cups of water? Is there hope to fully eradicate it with...

Been searching for something that works to get rid of fungus on my large toe for approximately 15 years. Fortunately has not spread to other toes. Tried Oregano Oil/Tea Tree Oil/ Vaseline/ Jublia for over 2+ years/Inpovidine etc. with no improvement. Now for approx 2 1/2 months I have been using a Mixture of apple cider vinegar-Himalayan salt & warm water applied x2 daily on recommendation of my podiatrist. I am finding the nail is...

My toes looked bad three weeks ago. I started soaking in apple cider vinegar and mouthwash 50-50 then after soaking very carefully cutting back the bad part of the nail with a flat razor blade. I've added colloidal silver with dropper rubbing it in waiting a bit then putting a light coating of antifungal cream then Vicks vapor rub and then I put socks on. I do it in the morning and night and also during the day once in a while. I also use a Dremel with an abrasive stone and grind it down once week. That seems to really help get the medicine in. The soaking will change the ph to a level that fungi does not like it will also kill any fungi you have on your foot, mine were dry and cracked and now they are much better. I also put anti fungal cream over all of my foot as well. It does not completely get rid of the fungi because when I stop being diligent it roars right back, usually over winter.

Hello, I bashed my toenail a few weeks ago it started to bleed and the blood went black under the nail. This toenail has already had mild fungus and it has never really grown back properly. Today I ripped the toenail off. Now the skin underneath is all white and looks rough how do I sort that out? Is that the fungus do I just apply ointment to that or do I need to cut the skin
I'm on oral medicine terbinafine and decided to get myself some things in Amazon. Foot soak epsom salts Listerine Anti fungal wash Tea tree oil See more

body: For those of you that had your right foot operated on, when were you able to drive after surgery?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: I waited the 6 weeks to drive locally. Drove long distance for the first time this Thursday and I'm at 3 months post op.
I had a cast for 8 weeks then a boot for two weeks. So, I drove after 10 weeks.
I've yet to have surgery but at my 1st consultation, I was told it would be 4 months before I could drive.
Legally, you're not allowed to drive if you have a boot on your foot
After my first bunion surgery, my doc gave me a driving shoe so I was able to drive at two weeks. I've recently had fusion surgery and I'm 3 weeks out and can't drive yet.
I was cleared to initially drive short distances, 10-15 mins from our house, once I was able to get my foot into a sneaker which was at 3 months post-op.
I waited 7 weeks. Need to be able to do an emergency stop safely or your insurance is invalid.
When I felt safe that I could perform an emergency stop without damaging my feet, bilateral 6th Jan drove short distances 18th Feb.
8 weeks had both feet done
I was told 6 weeks - see my surgeon this Thursday for the 6 week check so praying he says I'm good to go! Ready to get my life back now.

body: Not sure if this is any help to anyone. I went to a podiatrist for fungal nail treatment. He didn't think it was fungal so shaved down removed debris and gave me the Emtrix. I have used for 8 weeks daily and gone back. He has shaved down again and thinks I now have normal healthy nail to grow up. I am not convinced.

Hi everyone! Do y'all think this is fungus? Backstory: half my toe nail fell off about 1-2 months ago due to what looked like fungus, it is growing back but it looks very purplish.. I don't have insurance to go to a podiatrist, but I'd like your opinion. Also, would it be safe to put an acrylic toe nail over it so I can wear sandals?

Is it painful having the big toe nail removed? I'm scared. Edited to say I have a fungal big toe I banged it and it lifted and bled. I'm waiting to see the podiatrist and they said they'd have to remove it so I don't know if it's permanent or not yet. I'm just scared to have the bandage taken off and anything done as it's so sensitive.
comments: I had both of my big toenails removed at the exact same time and I will tell you it sucked the first few days. I was limping bad. But it is totally worth it and my fungus did not grow back with the nails fortunately.
Take some ibuprofen with you. When the numbness wore off it was bad until ibuprofen kicked in
Had mine done in January... didn't feel it while they were doing it but it was very sore in the days to follow. So far so good with it healing. Happy got it done.
First night is the worst get through that and you'll be fine.
Don't be scared, I've been through it and can tell you it's worth it in the end, getting mine removed early really helped and now I'm all good
Can I ask if it grows back or is it permanent?
A podiatrist will give you a local anaesthetic. It will be painless. Bit sore after local wears off. Take NSAIDs and Paracetamol if you're allowed. Temporary best if it's appropriate.
Are you doing temporarily or permanent? I had mine done permanent and it sucks.

I had one removed in 2022 it was very painful but Dr admitted it shouldn't have hurt that much and there's a possibility he didn't numb it enough as I felt everything during removal. It's grown back worse for me too.

Mine was surprisingly easy. Part of the removal did hurt, a lot. I didn't want to wait for another anesthetic injection to take effect so I just gritted my teeth for a few more seconds. After I was bandaged up, the recovery was fine.

body: Anyone have recommendations for toe/barefoot shoes like the ones below that will actually fit a bunion foot? I wear Topos for running and daily life which work well, but I'd like a pair of these too. The ones in the only store nearby that sells them are too narrow for my bunion.
Has anyone tried Hobibear barefoot shoes?

Anyone that has tried barefoot shoes (not necessarily the make) has it made any difference to your bunion? I'm wondering because I don't have that much movement in my big toe, if it will make it worse but the rocker trainer I was wearing was causing me so much pain so I'm trying something new. Thanks all. I'm trying to prevent surgery and trying to use my big toe more to strengthen it.

Hey guys I'm a low income person with really bad bunion I just switched over to using wide toe barefoot shoes on Amazon they seem to help with some of my foot pain but im looking into surgery options any advice from anyone?

I have a mild bunion on my right foot and a high instep. It's made it very hard to find shoes that fit well and don't hurt my feet for a reasonable price. I've looked into several different types of barefoot shoe but I'm a little nervous to try them because they don't seem to have any kind of cushion. As a waitress, a mom, and a rancher, I'm on my feet constantly and need some cushion to be able to go from standing still to moving a lot.
I had bunion surgery 6 months ago, and now my second toe bothers me. It looks like it became a hammertoe since surgery. Is this common?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Anyone else had to re-do the surgery because the screw didn't stay in place?? I think I moved to much (it was my second foot so felt a bit over confident)
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: Yep! It dropped the ball of my foot and I'm having reconstructive surgery next Monday

That happens when we do too much walking when the surgeon said not to my screw was attached to the bone and he countersunk it, but I overdid it walking and a screw backed out a little not enough to be concerned he said, but he mentioned stay off of it

Crikey!!! Is this what can happen? At what stage can this happen until? Like time frame after operation? I'm a week past surgery and have just been weight bearing for about an hour and have now got serious new pains which I've not had before so a bit worried
Yes. Just had the redo 2 weeks ago. 7 months after surgery, two screws broke from the plate along the area of the big toe
I've just had a revision done yesterday the screw had fractured and moved and the bone set in the wrong place.
I had a non fusion of the bone and had to have the surgery redone within 3 months. New surgery included more hardware and a bone graph from my heel. I was non weight bearing a total of 6 months
Mine partially fused so they are removing the hardware on the 5th. I wish you all the best recovery!
Yes, I just had revision on March 9th. Screw was completely loose and bone never healed. Frustrating.
Okay hold up... I have a bump (two actually) where the incisions are... I thought it was just part of the healing process. Could that be the screws moving??

body: Is anyone else 8 weeks post op and is not able to walk yet and has stiffness in toes and swelling?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: I was still in a boot at 8 weeks. Started to walk in a shoe at 10 weeks but had a bad limp and it was painful. Started PT and I've had 6 sessions. It's made a huge difference. No limp, I'm walking on the treadmill with no pain. My doctor told me that I will notice the biggest improvement between 3-6 months. This surgery will definitely test your patience. I'm at 16 weeks and finally feeling normal.
Almost 9 weeks post op. I am walking almost normally, but still mild discomfort when applying full pressure on the big toe side. I had lapiplasty. My ankle was and still is slightly weak
Have you went through physical therapy? Definitely going through physical therapy and religiously doing the exercises will help with better mobility.

Yes! I also had ankle surgery too which I'm sure isn't helping. Go tonight to pt eval. Having difficulty moving my toes and they aren't staying in place. Was given a tens unit to try and wake up the nerves too. I'm still in a boot mainly cause I have swelling and can't get into a shoe. But I've been doing some non boot time walking barefoot and need to use crutches to help off set the weight. Dr had me in an ace too to help with the swelling but told me I could stop that. I'm unsure that was the best move. Swelling is happening and now my ankle is sore and stiff.
I am 10 weeks and can walk. Going to PT ..morning stiffness is tough.
I'm 7 weeks. Course I have only been in a boot for 2 weeks with limited amount of time to be up (1 hour total) rest of time elevated. My big toe has some stiffness.
Surgery on Dec 17th. Walking okay. In PT to improve ROM of big toe.

body: Anyone else get a post-op splint that ended up being too tight? Surgery was Tuesday and I'm going into clinic tomorrow to get it changed out. Praying for no skin breakdown and a bit angry it wasn't assessed sooner. Was kept being told it was pain from the nerve block wearing off.. (I'm still numb in places). I'm convinced it's from the pressure and would have had much less pain had it been addressed sooner. Anywho- I'm a RN/NP
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Uh... did anyone else wear a toe spacer for a while and as a result get a pain in the middle top of the foot? I feel like I'm trading one issue for another.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Does anyone else have deep corn problems on the ball of the foot/ under bunion. It predates the start of the bunion which started about 10 yrs ago, the deep corn when I was in my twenties. I have to see a podologist every four weeks to be able to walk. In the last six months the bunion is hurting a lot more. Its time to do something. Im worried about the combination of the two for the healing. Hoping for MIS. I walk a lot, even with the pain.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: I had problems with hard firm deep corns under the ball of my feet and at the side of the bunions before my severe bunions were removed but that's one of the great things that disappeared after my surgery. They have completely gone\! You have just reminded me and I am thankful
I used to get these, I stopped walking round in bare feet, it worked for me.
Rachel Seth Thomas Sykes what? I cant walk barefoot, too painful
The black dots make me think this may actually be a wart
Aleena Naylor its blood spots
That's a plantar wart
may I suggest for now you switch to Altra shoes and correct toes toe spacer? This is because of the way your foot is taking the load. Wearing foot shaped shoes and the spacers could help that

Jacquelyn Dayton Berman Ive worn barefoot shoes the last three years, I think its the only way I can still walk distances, but theres no arch support (have tried adding stick in supports) the toe spacers are good sometimes but sometimes cause more pain under my big toe
The corn and the way you walk could be what caused the bunion. It's what causes callouses and corns are just really deep callouses.
Im also wondering if the corn has caused my bunion, makes sense, my foot lifting and twisting away from the pain
I use to get that on the bottom of my foot but after having a bunionectomy it went away
Catherine Hock Lamberti apparently mine is likely caused from the inside, bone protrusion so Im hoping surgery will end this misery too
Catherine Hock Lamberti how long did it take after your surgery for the calluses to go away?
Corn pad will help and you seem to need orthotics it seems. Something is rubbing on your toe also. It looks really sore. Find the best shoes money can buy that supports your needs.
Do your shoes have arch support? It might help.

body: Boric acid works\! Try it.

Treatment of house and shoes. Hello\! I am looking to know how to completely eradicate athlete's foot. It has been spread from my boyfriend to me and my son. We have carpet throughout the whole apartment. We also use the same tub sometimes. It's starting to stress me out. My boyfriend wears work boots that he leaves at work. I've heard of the apple cider vinegar soaks are effective. What are the ratios? 1 cup of vinegar to 3 cups of water? Is there hope to fully eradicate it with this approach?
3 years battling this. I've tried: Vinegar soaks, Vicks, Online Orivelle pens
Which Brooks (specifically) makes a wide toe box, without the thick cushion?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I just brought the Altra flow 2. They are very light and wide toe box. Really good in the heel. This is my first pair of this brand. Have pf for months and for the first time seeing a an improvement even after first day. The only thing that was sore were my legs but it was my first day using them.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Does anyone recommend Hike or Peak foot wear? Im looking for wide toe box, zero lift. I tried Hobibear, but not sure if I like them.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I just wanted to say that I completely understand that everybody's feet are different.... not everything works for everybody. But I think I have found what works for me. $39 Hobibear barefoot shoes on Amazon. Zero heel drop. Wide toe box. I'm walking without intense pain or limping. Am I healed completely? No, not yet. But this is a wonderful start. Thank you Jesus.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group
comments: Sounds like it's the shoes you should be thanking, not Jesus
Didn't work for me
Georgina Ungless I'm so sorry. Hokas didn't work for me. Oofos didn't either.
Do you wear orthotics with them?
Christina Stosney Congleton I put an insole in them but it's not got arch support
Are you still having heel pain? And they work for you?

I had some reels coming thru my feed about a month ago on barefoot shoes not like the ones I recall but as you describe with really wide toe box n zero drop that I need to research. And they look good lol. But lately I have started to do toe movement, stretches n exercises specifically for the bones of the foot n toes which has helped along with my calf exercises. Many good vids n reels out there
I started wearing barefoot shoes by Purestep, they along with regular strong calf massages cured my plantar fasciitis
Happy for you\! Zero drops were my answer.
I'm trying Altras. My pain has shifted from inner heel to middle heal..Trying to do Dr Angel stretches.

body: Planning to order Altra Lone Peak for trip where I will do some hiking. I usually order a wide shoe which usually means my heels tend to slip no matter using the runners lacing. Since Altras already have a wide toe box, should I try the regular first?

body: I had lapidus surgery on January 7th and I dont think I am 100% ready to return to work without restrictions or extended medical leave. I returned to work on 19th and 20th last week, putting in about 5 miles of steps each shift, which is about average, and about ready to cry mid shift in the bathroom as my left foot feels so sore, heavy, and refuses to bend. I was limping half way in on the first day and most of the day on Friday.

I'm in property management, so I can sit down as needed and, my foot is my driving foot. I'm just estimating like 3 weeks but wanted to see how it worked out for people who have already been through it.

body: 6 weeks and 4 days PO. Just finished my first day back at work. So glad I work from home\! I'm sore and it's a desk job\! While I couldn't elevate toes above nose, I was able to get it up a little, and ice my foot. It's still swollen and dark almost purple. Doesn't hurt though so I'm all good.

body: I went to a second opinion today\! After 3 surgeries on one foot, I found out it is nerve pain that's been the cause of all my pain\! I was told they will do steroid injections once a month for 3 months, plus to wear scar sheets and scar gel cream. Has anyone had nerve pain? And what have you done to help?

I am happy that I found this group, prompting me to do more research and get a second opinion. I went to a podiatrist a couple months ago and walked out uncertain about the procedure, doctor, and office staff. Today I saw an orthopedic foot surgeon who was very thorough, asked me questions about my lifestyle, health, and goals. The staff from front to back were professional and helpful. I walked out confident and ready to get my right foot done (left foot isn't as bad).

Im only 27 and im producing a very painful tailors bunion. My family doctor is saying not to do surgery but im looking for a second opinion bc in only 27 and its just going to get worse. My question is if i should do surgery or not? Do i do it now and get it out of the way and deal with the arthritis when im older or do the surgery when im older and risk infection and other issues. Idk what to do. A lot of people tell me not to do it and wait, but bc of that fact it doesn't make sense to me to wait? Help....

I have a bunion and hammertoe on my right foot. I just saw Podiatrist... No orthopedic surgeons nearby on my plan - I plan on getting a second opinion, but wanted to know if anybody here have any opinions. I have absolutely no pain in my bunion or my hammertoe. The only pain I get is rubbing on shoes. When I wear wide mesh shoes which are totally unattractive I'm OK. Podiatrist thinks I would most benefit from traditional bunion surgery.

I recently saw another doctor for a second opinion and they said the exact same thing. But this doctor showed me my X-ray and examined my arches and seemed much more thorough. I don't want to make the wrong choice with which doctor to use. How do I choose which one to go with. The first or the second?
2 days post surgery. I had the right foot done 10 yrs ago so knew what I was in for\!\! What i didnt expect was not to be given any crutches\!??\! Bloody gd job i have smart crutches

Victoria Rose Baxter asked me to keep you all updated on how my appointment went today. Week 4 after surgery, to the day, I got my stitches out\!\! Woo hoo\!\! I can walk with my boot but I have to say I am scared. It hurt so bad to put weight on it at first. This will take a bit. The best news.......I can shower without wearing the dang boot and actually sit on my shower chair by myself in my shower. I feel like I have died and gone to heaven.
6 Weeks post op\! Goodbye to the crutches & boot\! Hello to weight bearing and shoes\!

Hey yall\! I am having the lapiplasty operation on my left foot this June\! I had the Austin procedure done to my right foot in 2022. It was a long hard recovery. But the pain was manageable. Just took a long time for my foot to look normal nevertheless, it changed my life for the better\! And it's time to do the left\! I am in pain all day everyday\! I am wondering if anyone has had the two separate procedures on their feet? Or any insights?
Had surgery on Jan 16. Im very happy with the results. Before and after.

Update- I sent the picture to my foot doctor today and received a phone call back. He says it looks fine. I appreciate everyone's input. And I wish all of us on here a speedy/full recovery\! I had bunion surgery 18 days ago. I was told to take a shower today. I still can't put weight on that foot, it was a little scary. I have a few questions if anyone can give me their take post op. 1- it's the first time I actually looked at my foot.
comments: Shower stool. I sat and transferred into the shower then turned on water with handheld sprayer. Shower was awesome but yes purple color due to circulation. Its a good thing. Safety always first. It gets better. This is 6 weeks for me.
How red? My foot wasn't red. The big toe pain and numbness is normal.
That is terrific that you are 18 days out and your doctor is saying things are good! I had to see my podiatrist 3 days after surgery to get the dressing changed so I saw my foot.
We have a big walk in shower so I used a walker for support while I showered. One wall has a seat also so I sat for parts.
If you feel that there is a concern - can you take a pic and send it or do a telehealth visit? Trust your gut - better to be safe than sorry.
Everything is normal!!!
I bought a shower stool and sat on that with my cast covered from day 2. I then showered with my knee resting on the stool. I was non weight bearing for 6 weeks.
I'm 13 wks post op. Everything sounds normal. I wore my boot in the shower it helped a lot because it was painful in bare feet. Still is. Just double bag and tape your foot.
I have been showering since the third day after surgery and then about every other day. First, I bought a shower chair. Next, I have a walk in shower which has a hand held shower piece I can use.
Sponge bath

body: When you get a letter from your surgeon saying ...Sorry, never had a complication like yours before. Possible removal of plate and extra screw fixation ahead. Oh and to finish it off. 12 weeks NWB in full plaster.

After over a year my fungal nail is better\! What finally did it was a combination of washing all my clothes with dettol laundry cleaner in. Soaking in Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar and little borax a couple of time a week. Putting H-Nail Fungus and NonyX Nail gel on morning and night and regularly cleaning out under the nail and removing any fungal looking bits

Been fighting toenail fungus since 2002. I'm tired of buying drugs and creams that do little to nothing because I fail to take care of the real problem. Anyway, I just took my two foot tubs, filled them with freezing cold water, and put Dish soap and Crest Scope Mouth wash and started scrubbing. My first impressions were a success. The fungus was like a mucus, once I started scrubbing and getting off the problems my feet starts to get better.

Putting this out there in case it helps anyone. I battled for 20yrs with fungus infection with 3 of my toes on just my left foot. Drs had me try everything. Finally this week I was told to see a foot specialist. Little did I know since I had done everything already the only treatment left was to permanently remove them. I did. Best thing ever. They did it right in the office as a chemical removal. The worst part was the infection to numb it. Still healing but just using over the counter pain relief.
Hi my podiatry doctor has recommended hoka trainers as extra wide for my tailors bunion. Has anyone got these ? I'm in UK
comments: Hoka have a narrow toe box so interesting choice from your podiatrist. I've had a number of pairs but wore men's wide. Have you looked at Topo? I wouldnt wear anything else now. Naturally wide toe box

I recently tried several Hokas. Currently, all of them have a very tall plastic sole that wraps up and over the material. All models pinched the outer edge of my foot due to sitting on top of this edge. Their wides didn't feel much different.

Anyone else non weight barring? I'm supposed to be non weight barring for 1 more week!
Hello everyone. 54 almost 55. Had problems all my life.

UK here.
3rd week post double foot op. You all seem to know what was done to your feet. I am waiting until 6 week check to hear what actually happened in the surgery.
Have to say as a person who has had many operations this operation has thrown me. Patience is needed.
My before and after.  Surgery on 12/18.  Just had my 6 weeks.
6 week check up today all looks good doctor said bone is growing back. Go back in 5 weeks.

I'll be 6 weeks post op tomorrow (Monday) and have my final appt with the surgeon on Wednesday. I have a few questions for this knowledgable group. 
1-How long after surgery until you no longer had swelling and could fit into your pre op shoes? I don't have much swelling but I tried on my shoes last night and it felt very tight and my foot is tender. (I'm still using the surgical shoe but will most likely be told on Wednesday that I can start transitioning to regular shoes.)

I live in New England (close enough to Boston) that I'm looking for a malpractice attorney. I had a second opinion on my feet after 4 foot surgeries to find out the surgeon butchered my feet and I'm basically screwed for life. I'd rather do this annon because I don't need it getting back to the doctor. Thank you in advance.

body:
Injury: Hallux Limitus
Question: Looking for info on morton's extension orthotics
I saw my Sports MD again and he watched me walk again (note, I've been dealing with symptoms for 11 months with MANY misdiagnoses along the way). He feels my issues are related to Hallux Limitus of the big toe. My symptoms are with my first and second toes. My second toe feels very sore at the joint and sometimes swells up.

body:
I have to say this was the best experience! I was reading all the post op posts on here and was scared to death of the recovery after bunion surgery. Posts of so much pain, not being able to bear weight for several weeks, unable to move their big toe without alot of pain and no driving for several months. I cancelled my surgery.
Then I found Foot First Podiatry and Dr Sklar! Holy Moly! The absolute opposite of all the posts! Weight bearing the same day, not on the ball of my foot.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body:
For over a year now I've been working on going somewhat barefoot.
Seems it's not doing my feet any good. I do wear toe separators (not 100% of the time) and in that time have developed a tailors Bunion (left foot). Little toe still wants to move "in".
2nd and 3rd toe seem "displaced on both feet.
No I'm not wearing arrow shoes.
Have been doing foot exercises...guess it's off to the foot doctor...just hope he can help with prescribing very costly orthotics.

body: Update on the ten little piggies. Soaked today in Epsom Salt for 35 minutes. After drying, Dropper applications of bleach and Tea Tree oil, separately. Obviously they look rough but I can say there is improvement. Also noticed less pressure from the nail bed. Tonight, the treatment will be Dropper applications direct on the toes of bleach, tea tree oil, vicks. Cotton socks for 8 hours afterwards.
I guess slow progress is better than no progress. This is about 60 days of brushing bleach on my toenails.
Hey all I've heard that diluting bleach with water and brushing some on your toenail and under your nail can help eliminate fungus. Has anyone tried or heard this before? Thanks

This is after 1 year of treatment with Jublia and I used other things as well. Such as tea tree oil, bleach soaks and applying bleach directly. What helped most was grinding down the nails to almost nothing so the medicine could reach the nail bed. As you can see all toes are almost healed except the big toe I'm still working on and a little toe in the left foot. I also wear sandals everyday.
Has anyone tried taking soaking their toenail in bleach? Or even taking niacin orally?

body: I walk 18km then the next day my left foot is severly painful i think its sesamoiditis what should i do? i cant afford to go to the hospital

Is there such a thing as a big toe joint replacement? I have been dealing with chronic pain for years as a result of botched bunionectomy. The botched surgery left me with horrible sesamoiditis, which eventually led to a sesamoidectomy (can't recall at the moment whether it was tibial or medial, but it did nothing for the ball of foot pain). I have very little hope, but always hoping that there will be medical advances for foot surgery.

I have had plantar faciitis & sesamoiditis on and off for years. In the past, used ice, tennis ball, stretches & it calmed down. Now for the past 19 months I have had symptoms of both. I have been in Physical Therapy, seen 3 podiatrists, 4 cortisone shots. PF is better but still ball of foot pain, have tried insoles (insurance will not pay for custom orthotics). Now I have stopped gym & fitness walking. I am at my wit's end.
Help!!! Has anyone experienced sesamoiditis, and if so, how have you resolve it?
Can gout cause sesamoiditis? Any tips when dealing with both problems at the same time

Alright update.
Well the doctor is going to put me on gabapentin but he is going to get me a referral to see the neurologist that's in the same building. He will conduct a nerve test on me but also look at my back. Because I did ask him if my back problems could also be related to my feet problems and he said yes that's a possibility. He basically says it could be tarsal tunnel with it only happening with activity.

hey there - newbie here. So 5 yrs ago had knee surgery (microfracture) and after that my gait changed causing all sorts of tendinitis (perennial and Achillies). To add insult to injury, I put on compression sleeves to help, but all it did was cause my tarsal tunnel to get inflamed in both feet which took over a year to calm down from. For years now my feet have been hypersensitive to shoes/sneakers. Any new sneaker/shoe I feel even a seam in.

I've been diagnosed with Plantar Fasciitis & Sinus Tarsi syndrome & chronic postoperative pain... Surgical history tarsal coalition resection and excision of anterior process of the calcaneus fracture fragment. What helped.. Steroid shots which only provided transient benefit for a week.. I may be a candidate for DRG. But all these what ifs.. back to working full time hours. I need to work. But it's so hard to have the drive and continue.
Has anyone here been diagnosed with Tarsal tunnel syndrome?

Anyone with Tarsal Tunnel Syndrome?
Can you tell me where your pain/swelling is
Thank you

Hi
I removed my big toe nail 2 months ago. I was soaking and doing all things as Dr told me. I am using ketocanozole ointment for the last one month. I still soak the toes in warm water once every 2 days.
I was covering my toe with band aid for most of the time. But Dr told me to keep it open and been doing it only for the last few days.

I have been fighting toenail fungus just on my one big toe for about 5 years now. My husband tells me I just need to have it removed. Which I have made an appointment for June to do so. I'm just wondering if you have had your nail removed and it been successful?
So I have an appointment booked with the podiatrist. I'm getting my nails removed.
I was looking at old posts to see if I saw an answer to this but my toenail fell off today. It's been loose for a while. Does this look normal?

Has anyone had toenail removed and take forever to heal? It's been 1.5 months so far. Infected. Throbbing bad. Antibiotics previously. Cream. Soaks. Mind you same thing happened on other foot 15 years ago. It got so bad my toe turned black and had to go to wound clinic to debris. I was literally 23. I'm nervous same thing gonna happen.
I do have Raynauds which causes circulation issues I think is factor. I'm not diabetic.

Feeling like my old self again and going to start hiking again.

comments: Don't waste your money.
I have and it's not effective at all!!
Tape your feet with zinc oxide tape
My son is college pitcher and had it done in his shoulder without success, but I think it just depends. He's had teammates have success. Give it a try!
Following.. in the same boat!
I've had this done on my left foot for Plantar Fasciitis and it was fantastic! Had it done in 2019 and to this day no issue with PF in that foot.
I done this for my elbow helped for a year or so. I have PF have for years have tried everything except that or surgery nothing works

The tendons and ligaments around my wife's hip was causing her very bad pain. She did everything the doctor recommended and nothing helped. He told her that a PRP injection has helped some but not everyone. She decided to try it. 3 weeks later the pain began to subside and now she's pain free.
A friend did it for an elbow thing. Said he's pain free and very happy with PRP.
Evidence shows that progressive strengthening of the foot and other relevant muscles is the most effective way to treat PF, and prevent it from ever returning.

body: Does the treadmill make anyone else's pf flare up and burn? I want to exercise but I suffer for days if I push myself. What other exercises is good that won't make pf flare up?
comments: Yes. My doctor said no treadmill. I do the exercise bike and yoga

The treadmill is one of the worst things that you can be on because every single solitary step is exactly the same one right after the other. The alternative would be either a really good exercise bike or what I think is the best of the best which is an elliptical.
At physical therapy I would start on a bike for 5 minutes to get the blood moving. I usually stayed on it longer
Elliptical, NuStep, Bike, Swimming, weights, Rower.
Yep, switched to the bike for a year. Hated it. But necessary. I bought my own seat to bring to the gym
I had to switch to swimming
Treadmill use was one of the reasons I have pf. It's very bad for pf. Elliptical trainer has been my go to
An incline over 3 will do it.
Unfortunately treadmills don't have the same force absorption as the ground does. I would avoid the treadmill until your PF subsides.
I was thinking of trying a rowing machine. I heard it is great exercise! Anyone tried rowing with pf?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I don't know if this may help anyone else but recently I have been soaking my feet in very hot water, with Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar and tea tree oil. My pain is receding and I am managing to run 12 miles a week slowly.
comments: What is the proportion for apple cider, epsom and tea tree oil? thanks
U had only inflammation?
How much time soaking
How much of the Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar, and tee tree oils do you use? How long do you soak them?
Yes! I did this as well and it helped a Lot! After being in pain n barely walking for 6 months or so, my feet have been great for past 3 months!
Hot Epson water is the only thing that relieved my pain.
Everybody talks about their heel hurting, but mine is sore right in the middle of my foot so do you all think that is PF?
I'm gonna try this.
I do the graston technique on my feet and calves after soaking mine in a very hot Epsom salt bath. TenJet got me to the 90% mark and this got me to the 100% mark.
Epsom salt works for mine

I got my cortisol shot today, or whatever was in that needle, right here because that is where the pain is concentrated.
My pain is mostly in the heel
Yes. Its all connected to calf muscle
Could be heel spurs. That's one of issues.
My pain has always been in my heel and sometimes radiates higher up
Yep, that's where mine is. It is excruciating in the morning and painful during the day
Yes, that is the last hold out for pain for me. I have had shots, multiple rounds of PT and the tenex procedure and that is the pain that remains no matter what I do.
Yes, have had heel spurs. The only thing you can do for them is put something like Dr. Scholl half size heel pads in the heel area of your shoes.
I got professional massages after 3 rounds of cortisone shots. Tell them you have PF. Mine is gone. Lived with it for 5 years.

body: Hi everyone, Had foot nail fungus for 17 years now. I'm pretty positive I caught it with my mom's contaminated nail polish and clippers. I wasn't aware. It started on my left big toe and then it spread like wildfire to the other. I also had AF and my feet were itchy. So never took medical care because I was ashamed. I went years without exposing my feet. I was afraid of vacations and yoga classes. Anything that involves exposing my feet. I'm 33 and I said no more to this embarrassment. I have insurance and I decided to see a good Foot and ankle doctor. She took clipping sent it to a lab and then said Terbinafine is the answer for 12 weeks.

Just a note for those considering oral meds, I didn't have a single side effect taking it for 3 months and my liver wasn't affected at all, it stayed normal. I am religious so I don't drink alcohol or coffee and never have done drugs so my liver is likely better than most. I did have to take pain meds on my period which I was worried about but still no side effects. My nail fungus was mild but had lasted over 7 years.
Which home remedies have you tried, and did they actually work for you?
comments: Vicks worked for me and many others
Nonyx nail gel, dettol in your washing. Regularly filing and cleaning out nail. Epsom salts and apple cider vinegar foot soaks.

I've had this going on for 5 years plus, I've tried everything and nothing worked so I hear every one talking about Vicks vapor rub and gave it a shot. U have to get rid of the nail by grinding it down or clipping it off and I use peroxide in warm water, put my feet in and soak about 10 min then put on Vicks and socks and go to bed, I can tell it's working!

I was on Reddit awhile back trying to look at new ways and someone posted Betadine/iodine 10% solution. You have to cut the nails back really short and then file them until they are a bit porous and then apply twice a day for a few months maybe longer depending on level of infection.
None
Vinegar. 5 toes had it. It's taken Almost 2 years. 4 toes clear... big toe close. But better than anything else I've tried in 15 years including meds.. prescriptions.. Vicks... nothing else worked.

I've tried so many, some worked a little, but nothing cured it until I finally just took terbinafine. I partially procrastinate because the topical version did nothing, but the oral medication worked.
Vicks works if you apply a nice amount and cover it with a band-Aid.

body: I'm scheduled for surgery this Saturday. I've been reading through posts to get an idea of how I'm going to feel/manage. I like to think I push pain to one side and just get on with things however I know that I'll definitely need rest and elevation. I've given myself a 2 week schedule to switch off. I'm office based and plan to go back to work, sedentary after 2 weeks. Am I being unrealistic? How was your pain in the first few days? How long until weight bearing? Did it drive you crazy doing nothing?
comments: What procedure are you having? I had a lapidus last year and worked from home for 15 weeks. I am 10 weeks post op on my second lapidus and still home.
I had Lapidus along with osteotomy on three toes. I only needed pain meds the first two days. I work from home and was able to return to work the second week. I did elevate while sitting at my desk.
My surgery was 2/25. My pain was manageable as I kept on top of it. The first few days I alternated between pain meds and Tylenol and then after about 5 days I only took Tylenol.
I managed 4 days taking my pain medication religiously before I had to get up and move about and it was the best decision I made even with pins in my toes.
It all depends on your non weight bearing length of time, if you are in plaster or not. I'm 10 days post op in plaster NWB for 6 weeks.

It depends on the surgery you are having and whether you are able to be weight bearing. I had lapiplasty which was pretty invasive and I just started walking at 8 weeks, driving at 9 because it was the right foot.
It depends on the procedure. I had a chevron osteotomy and I had my foot wrapped for 4 weeks and was advised to elevate for that entire time.
I took two weeks off work and spent that time resting, reading, binged one series, and adjusting to the lack of mobility as an active person. Knee scooter was a lifesaver.
First foot I had to be carried to bathroom for days, and was on pain meds for 5 weeks. Second foot walked from start and can take the dog out from week 2.
I had lapiplasty. I could walk using my heel in a boot in a few weeks. The pain only lasted about 5 days.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: The supportive shoe vs barefoot shoes conundrum... I have and use both kinds, but I feel better using support because the floors I walk on all day are super hard. body: Looking for anyone's experience with this product or with tea tree oil for getting rid of toenail fungus. Has either product worked for you? Im desperate
Castor oil, is it worth trying? If you use this, how are you applying it, and does it have to be the organic one?

Does anyone else find relief with Oofos slides? My pain was manageable until I purchased these slides. That's when I noticed that when I wore them, I felt much better BUT overall the pain started increasing. I had to stop going to the gym even. I still wear them because they are the only slides that are so comfortable——but I'm wondering if they are making the healing process slower.
comments: All I wear are Oofos and sneakers

My PF is starting to disappear. However, my outer ankle on my bad foot has been a problem. I'm sure it's because I have been limping and walking improperly for so long. My Oofos seem to aggravate my ankle situation.
They are so comfortable but put my PF into a stretched position that flares mine.

I wore mine when my pain was at its worst. As my pain improved, while wearing regular shoes, after I lost some weight and was on anti inflammatories I didn't wear them as much. I, too, noticed they were sometimes adding to my pain after wearing them.

So a few orthopedic surgeons and podiatrists tell me that OOFOS are exactly what they say they are. RECOVERY shoes. They said they are meant to be worn for painful days, not EVERYDAY. Not supportive enough, and too soft and pliable. Pretty much they intensify your problem if worn daily.
Nah they are rubbish, don't get the comments.
Its not wise to wear just one type of shoe for days. You need to swap out. You need to switch shoes out daily, every couple days - your feet will tell you.
I find them only good when I'm extremely inflamed and just for a short time
Made me worse
If it's painful, how is it comfortable to you?

body: I wanted to share this for anyone in here who feels like there's no way out of plantar fasciitis right now. It can get better. But it takes time, consistency, and patience. I've been dealing with it since January 2025. Like a lot of people, I tried to push through it at first - kept running until around August when it finally stopped me in my tracks and had me limping.
comments: This is really positive; thanks for sharing
May I ask how you do the calf stretches and what runners were you using along with the Oofos?
Thank you for the encouraging words! This all seems so hopeless sometimes. Did you have setbacks along your recovery journey?
Never underestimate the power of the big toe!!!
I'm finally glad to see someone with results after strengthening the foot. Everyone seems to stretch but not strength train. I'm doing things similar to you. I am also a runner.
This really made me feel better! I think a lot of my issues stem from tight calves and weakness in my glutes, calves and hips.
Thank you for sharing. Will try!
Thanks so much for the detailed guidance. You have me feeling hopeful.
Good advice. It's a marathon not a sprint. This developed over time and requires time to heal with proper exercise and treatment.
Thanks so much for the ideas and mainly the hope!

I've been on several cruises but each time i was scared. I've always put on a fake toenail on the infected nail and then clip the fake nail with nail clippers into my regular nail shape then paint over it. What makes me scared is them popping off and has happened at dinner before.
I have a full set of acrylic toenails for events
Look into Keryflex

I went on a cruise and have the same issue. I went to my nail salon and she put acrylic on my bad nails and made them look great! I felt normal for the first time in years. When I got back, I took them off.
I have a friend that has fake big toenails put on every month at the nail salon. Maybe?
I paint over my manky toe nails with bright purple nail varnish

body: Anyone dealt with tailors bunion.? I can feel the bone just a little down from my little toe & my little toe hurts on the side .. any at home relief ?
comments: I just had a Tailor's appear overnight, red and so sore I could not wear shoes. So doc gave me a steroid shot (painful!) but it really worked. Bone still protruding the same! How long does this steroid shot work?

Tailor's bunions tend to get callouses, which hurt. Callouses are prone to warts. If you get all 3 together you need to see a GOOD podiatrist. A wart can look like a callous and you think it's just a callous.
Get it fixed. My tailors bunion was so painful after working on my feet all day. Glad I did. Easy surgery
I use toe spreaders and a wide toe box shoe like Altra running shoes. Then use some ibuprofen to knock down inflammation. It then goes away, takes couple weeks but works.
